name: Oaxaca (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Oaxaca centred.
history: Oaxaca has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms feature the severed arms of the Princess Donaji rising from the earth (a Zapotec legend), surrounded by quartered emblems recalling the indigenous heritage, the Spanish colonial period (the city's title "Muy Noble y Muy Leal"), and the natural and agricultural wealth of the state.
